project_management_remaining_courses.title: IT Project Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Specialized project management techniques for information technology projects including software development, system implementations, and technology infrastructure projects. Students learn to manage the unique challenges of IT project delivery.
learning_objectives: Apply project management to software development lifecycles
Manage technology infrastructure and system implementation projects
Understand IT-specific risks and mitigation strategies
Coordinate technical teams and stakeholder requirements
Implement quality assurance and testing in IT projects
covered_materials: IT risk management and security considerations

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Construction Project Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Project management principles applied to construction and engineering projects including planning, scheduling, and coordination of construction activities. Students learn industry-specific practices and regulatory requirements.
learning_objectives: Apply project management to construction and engineering projects
Understand construction scheduling and resource management
Manage construction contracts and procurement
Implement safety and quality management in construction
Navigate regulatory and environmental requirements
covered_materials: Construction scheduling and critical path method

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Project Management Tools and Software
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Comprehensive study of project management software tools and technologies including planning, scheduling, collaboration, and reporting tools. Students gain hands-on experience with industry-standard project management applications.
learning_objectives: Master project management software applications
Use collaboration and communication tools effectively
Implement project reporting and dashboard solutions
Evaluate and select appropriate PM tools for different contexts
Integrate multiple tools for comprehensive project management
covered_materials: Time tracking and resource management tools
Integration and data exchange between tools

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Change Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Systematic approach to managing organizational change in project contexts including change planning, stakeholder engagement, and resistance management. Students learn to facilitate successful organizational transitions.
learning_objectives: Develop comprehensive change management strategies
Assess organizational readiness for change
Implement change communication and training programs
Manage resistance and facilitate change adoption
Measure and sustain change initiatives

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Contract Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Comprehensive study of contract management throughout the project lifecycle including contract development, negotiation, administration, and closeout. Students learn legal and business aspects of project contracting.
learning_objectives: Develop and structure project contracts effectively
Negotiate contract terms and conditions
Administer contracts and manage performance
Handle contract changes and disputes
Understand legal and risk aspects of contracting
covered_materials: Change order management and contract modifications
Legal aspects of contracts and liability

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Project Management Ethics
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Ethical considerations and professional responsibilities in project management including stakeholder interests, resource allocation, and decision-making frameworks. Students develop ethical reasoning skills for project contexts.
learning_objectives: Understand ethical frameworks and principles in project management
Analyze ethical dilemmas in project decision-making
Apply professional codes of conduct and standards
Balance competing stakeholder interests ethically
Develop personal ethical guidelines for project practice
covered_materials: PMI Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
Transparency and honesty in project reporting
Conflict of interest and professional integrity

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Performance Measurement and Control
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Systems and methodologies for measuring and controlling project performance including metrics development, monitoring techniques, and corrective action processes. Students learn to implement effective project control systems.
learning_objectives: Develop comprehensive project performance measurement systems
Implement project monitoring and control processes
Use earned value management for performance analysis
Create meaningful project dashboards and reports
Apply corrective and preventive action processes
covered_materials: Project control system design and implementation

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Organizational Behavior
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Study of individual and group behavior in organizational settings including motivation, leadership, communication, and organizational culture. Students learn to understand and influence behavior for improved organizational effectiveness.
learning_objectives: Understand individual behavior and personality in organizations
Analyze group dynamics and team effectiveness
Apply motivation theories to improve performance
Understand organizational culture and change processes
Develop skills in leadership and influence
covered_materials: Individual differences and personality in organizations

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Business Process Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Systematic approach to analyzing, designing, and improving business processes including process mapping, optimization, and automation. Students learn to enhance organizational efficiency through process improvement.
learning_objectives: Analyze and map business processes effectively
Identify process improvement opportunities
Design optimized business processes
Implement process automation and technology solutions
Measure and monitor process performance

project_management_remaining_courses.title: Financial Management
project_management_remaining_courses.description: Fundamental principles of financial management including financial analysis, planning, and decision-making. Students learn to analyze financial performance, manage working capital, and make investment and financing decisions.
learning_objectives: Analyze financial statements and performance ratios
Develop financial plans and budgets
Make capital budgeting and investment decisions
Understand working capital and cash flow management
Evaluate financing options and capital structure
covered_materials: Time value of money and present value concepts
Capital budgeting techniques and decision criteria
Cost of capital and financing decisions
